canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, ImageNet-R is consistently described as a benchmark for evaluating model performance. The most common framing positions it as a tool for assessing model robustness to out-of-distribution images. A related but more specific definition from one paper states it is designed to test generalization to novel styles. The benchmark's content is consistently characterized as consisting of various artistic renditions of ImageNet object classes. Examples provided in the source materials include 'paintings, cartoons, sculptures' and 'embroidery'.
papers: Frozen Transformers in Language Models Are Effective Visual Encoder Layers
DEEM: Diffusion models serve as the eyes of large language models for image perception
LiNeS: Post-training Layer Scaling Prevents Forgetting and Enhances Model Merging
Mitigate the Gap: Improving Cross-Modal Alignment in CLIP
Transformer Meets Twicing: Harnessing Unattended Residual Information

definitions.text: An image classification benchmark consisting of various artistic renditions (e.g., paintings, cartoons, sculptures) of ImageNet object classes, designed to test generalization to novel styles.
A benchmark for evaluating model robustness to out-of-distribution images, containing artistic renditions of ImageNet objects.
definitions.paper: Frozen Transformers in Language Models Are Effective Visual Encoder Layers
